title: Use-case declaration
x-awen-status: reconciled 2026-07-04 — constitution-layer SOV-002 transparency artefact; no awen-weave craidd validator (declaration, not a canonical-store write). Shape unchanged.
description: A community's DECLARATION of how it uses its own data (SOV-002). This is a transparency + sharing artefact, NOT a permission request. There is no approved/denied state. Filed to the community-facing register and federated to a shared catalogue.

declarant.description: Who is declaring (verified attestor, as per the proposal envelope's attestor block).

instance.description: The community/instance whose data this concerns (e.g. dolgellau-town-dataset, gwynedd-community-mapping).

data_scope.description: High-level description of which data is used (entity/claim types, tier). Not a query; a human-readable scope statement for transparency.

purpose.description: What the community is doing with the data and why.

benefit_to_community.description: How this benefits the community. If financial, how returns flow to the community (e.g. via the CIC/steward).

financial.description: Whether the use generates financial benefit. Financial is legitimate (SOV-002) — declared, not gated.

shareable.description: May this use case be shared to the central catalogue for other communities to learn from / reuse? (Sharing the idea, not the data.)

lessons.description: Optional: what was learned, for the shared catalogue.

outcome.description: What the use case achieved. Drives the push mechanism (SOV-002).

impact.description: Self-assessed; high + shareable triggers push consideration.

domain.description: Domain for push matching, e.g. health, environment, youth.

push.description: If true and shareable, actively surfaced to matching communities rather than left to passive browse.

external_data.description: Set when the use case relies on third-party / proprietary data accessed under a separate agreement (SOV-003). The transparency floor (SOV-004) applies: data_schema is ALWAYS recorded; only detail may be secured. There is no hidden tier.

agreement_kind.description: constitutional = struck via the Awen data-access instrument (shareable as a pattern); transparency-floor = holder accepts the SOV-004 floor (schema recorded, detail secured) but not full constitutional governance. A holder refusing even the floor is declined — no enum value, because such a use is not permitted (SOV-004).

data_schema.description: REQUIRED (transparency floor, SOV-004): the schema/structure/kind of the external data in use — what it is, always recorded, even when detail is secured. Concealing this is not a permitted state.

agreement_ref.description: Reference to the agreement instrument/template used (the shareable route), NOT the data.

holder_shareable.description: Whether the data holder's identity may be named publicly. Detail-level; the schema is recorded regardless.

data_holder.description: Named only if holder_shareable is true.

confidential_note.description: Pointer that confidential DETAIL (values, terms, identity) exists and is held privately; never the detail itself, and never a substitute for data_schema.

status.description: Lifecycle ONLY. Deliberately no 'pending-approval' / 'approved' / 'denied' — this is declaration, not permissioning (SOV-002).
